Monthly Highlights • May 2026

My May 2026 as a Google Student Ambassador

✍️ Monisha Kumar 📍 SMVEC, Puducherry 🪪 GID: 5429 📅 May 2026
Chapter 01

A Missed Deadline and a Midnight Decision

Late April 2026. I applied for the Google Student Ambassador Program. On May 2nd, an email landed in my inbox — I had cleared the first round. Shortlisted. Out of thousands.

Except I didn't see it. I was neck-deep in semester examinations, and by the time I surfaced, the 72-hour deadline for the second round video submission had already passed.

"I felt terrible. But something in me refused to give up."

After a very long internal debate — will they even consider me? Is it too late? What's the point? — something clicked. I got up at midnight, prepared my script, recorded my video, and sent it across with an email that was equal parts request and apology.

Then I prepared my mind for either outcome. Rejection or selection — I was ready for both.

Chapter 02

The Notification That Stopped the Scroll

May 11th. Regular morning. I opened my phone to scroll Instagram — and stopped cold. A notification. An email from the Gemini Program Team.

"You didn't just show up. You showed range." — The Gemini Program Team

I was officially a Google Student Ambassador 2026. Selected. After a late submission. After a midnight recording. After almost giving up.

When I told my HOD, she was genuinely happy and proud. My department staff congratulated me. And in that moment I realised something — our staff aren't just teachers. They are our biggest support pillars. They teach us, guide us, support us, and celebrate us.

Chapter 03

Getting Official — One Email at a Time

After the selection, the onboarding began. Here's how May unfolded:

MAY 13

Portal Access & GID Assigned

Official access to Ambassador portal. My unique identity: GID 5429.

MAY 19

WhatsApp Community Access

Finally joined the regional GSA community channel.

MAY 26

First Ambassador Meeting

Attended the GSA Orientation Session via Google Meet with 260+ ambassadors!

Chapter 04

Building My Ambassador Identity

Once I was official, I got to work building my presence on campus. First things first — I needed a community hub for SMVEC students.

I created a WhatsApp group called TeamGemini@SMV — a dedicated space for GSA communication on our campus. 25 members and growing. I also designed the logo using Gemini itself. Meta? Absolutely.

Join TeamGemini@SMV WhatsApp Channel

Next up — my Ambassador ID card. Because if you're representing Google on campus, you better look the part. I designed it myself and I'm quite proud of it.

I also visited multiple departments across our college — meeting students, talking to staff, building connections. Usually I'm not the most outgoing person. But the ambassador role quietly pushed me out of my comfort zone.

Chapter 05

Hosting My First Webinar — 7 Great Hearts

May's theme: "Your Dream Career." My task: host a webinar with minimum 30 participants. My result: 7 showed up.

💡 Fun fact: I invited ~30 people. 7 attended. And those 7 made it the best event it could have been. Quality over quantity, as they say.

The event was a Career Glow-up Night — a fun pitch night where participants used Google Gemini to write their personal brand packaging copy and Nano Banana to bring it to life visually. Then they pitched themselves to the room in 60 seconds. Buy It or Leave It style.

After the event, I personally edited and designed participation certificates for each attendee and sent them individually via email. Because every person who showed up deserved to be recognized properly.

🎓 Certificates issued: Each participant received a personalized Participation Certificate, personally edited and emailed by me. The top prompter received a special Achievement Certificate.

The honest feedback I received from participants was gold:

1
Improve communication and storytelling — Don't let the session become monotonous. Keep the energy alive throughout.
2
Connect with more people — Wider network = bigger impact. Start before the event, not during.
3
State the goal clearly — Tell people what they're walking into from the very first message.

"Those 7 people changed my perspective about the people around me. I usually don't talk to many people. That evening, I did."

Chapter 06

The Part That Peaked Everything — Campus Interviews

If one thing truly defined my May as an ambassador, it was the campus interviews.

I interviewed 10 students — people who are building projects, running ventures, working on something entrepreneurial. All via Google Meet, since semester exams made in-person meetings impossible.

10
Students Interviewed
5
Min Per Interview
∞
Stories Discovered

And I got to know so much. About their ideas. Their struggles. Their vision. The most incredible minds are quietly working around us every single day — and most of us never ask them about it.

"You don't know your campus until you start asking questions."

Chapter 07

Content, Connections and an Unexpected Beginning

For the content creation task, I posted my first Instagram reel showing how Gemini can help students build their dream career — CV support, interview prep, research. A classmate helped me edit the video and I'm genuinely grateful for that hand.

I published a LinkedIn post to go with it. Connected with fellow ambassadors across India. Built a small but real network of people doing similar work in different corners of the country.

And then — something I didn't plan for — I started writing.

"I've always wanted to write my own blog. The GSA program gave me the push I needed. This post is proof of that."

This very blog post is that beginning. And I intend to keep going.

Chapter 08

May 2026 — In Numbers and Memories

One month. More than I expected.

What May 2026 held 🗓️

✍️ Wrote 8 semester exams including 1 Honours paper
🎓 Completed 3rd year of Engineering
🚀 Selected as Google Student Ambassador
🎙️ Hosted my first webinar
🎬 Posted my first Instagram reel
🤝 Interviewed 10 student entrepreneurs
🏫 Visited multiple departments, built new connections
📝 Started writing my blog
📈 Upgraded to Final Year SMVECian
💼 Started Placement Training

Grateful to the Universe for that midnight spark.

As an ambassador, May 2026 was huge. I learned, I pushed boundaries, I found where I need to grow, and I improved — even if just a little. But most of all, I am grateful for the spark that made me get up, record that video, and send it when I had almost lost hope. That spark changed everything.

MONISHA KUMAR • GOOGLE STUDENT AMBASSADOR 2026 • GID: 5429 • SMVEC, PUDUCHERRY
